title: tragopogon conduplicatus
intro: Tragopogon conduplicatus, also known as the Creeping Salsify or Creeping Jack, is a perennial plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea, and as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce swelling and pain. The health benefits of Tragopogon conduplicatus include its ability to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion by steeping its roots and leaves in hot water, and it can also be used topically as a poultice or ointment to accelerate healing and reduce inflammation.
